Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Forder Imposing Terminating Sanction Of Dismissal With Prejudice And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-27-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Gary Zahlen's Motion For Terminating Sanction denied. The Court orders sanctions as follow: (1) Plaintiff is precluded from introducing into evidence any documents described in, or that are responsive to, defendant Zahlen's Request For Production of Documents [Set One]; (2) All objections to defendant Zahlen's Form Interrogatories and Request For Production of Documents [Set One] are deemed waived, including attorney-client and attorney work product privileges; and (3) monetary sanctions shall issue in the sum of $1,347.50. Commissioner: PAUL SLAVIT

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-30-2006 Continued To Jul-24-06 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-13-2006 Continued To Jun-12-06 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212
MINI MINUTES: Philip T. Kilduff, Esq., appearing specially for Plaintiff. The OSC is re-set to 6/12/2006 at 1:30 PM, in Department 212. Proceedings reported by: Helen Sorep-Matley CSR 225. (212)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling [Further] Responses To Form Interrogatories, To Request For Production Of Documents, And To Request For Admissions, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-14-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories, request for production of documents, and requests for admissions is granted. No opposition has been filed. The objections, other than those based on the attorney client or attorney work product privilege are overruled and stricken. Plaintiff is ordered to provide further verified responses and produce documents within 21 days of notice of this order. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $790 are to be paid within 21 days of notice of this order. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN
